title: Improve Error Handling in Metrics Lambda Service 1
description:
The Metrics Service 1 Lambda was returning a generic internal server error during customer use case testing. The root cause was that the API request to the Lambda endpoint and the subsequent database write were combined in a single try-catch block, making it impossible to distinguish between external API failures and internal database errors.

## Required Change
Separate the error handling into two distinct try-catch blocks:
1. **API request block** — captures errors from the external Lambda service call
2. **Database write block** — captures true internal errors from inserting/updating `MetricApiResponse` and `ApiResponseWidget` records

This allows the system to return meaningful error messages to the user rather than a generic 500 error.
acceptance_criteria: API request to the Lambda endpoint has its own try-catch block with specific error messaging
Database insert/update operations have a separate try-catch block
API errors return the Lambda service error message to the frontend
Database errors return a distinct internal error message
Widget data is correctly parsed and saved to ApiResponseWidget table on success
middleware_changes: Refactored the Metrics Service 1 controller to separate the Lambda API call and database write into distinct try-catch blocks. Error messages are now specific to the failure point rather than generic.
implementation_notes: The original code combined the HTTP POST to the Lambda endpoint (https://hkv22n88tc.execute-api.us-west-2.amazonaws.com/dev) and the MetricApiResponse/ApiResponseWidget database writes in one try-catch. The refactored code isolates each operation for precise error reporting.
comment_summary: Waqar deployed the updated error handling to the staging environment.
